part.text:
# File Search Endpoint with ApiDeck File Storage (OneDrive)

## Background

You are working with the Apideck File Storage Unified API connected to OneDrive. Your job is to seed the configured OneDrive drive with four marker files and then exercise the unified `Search Files` endpoint to retrieve only the relevant subset.

## Requirements

* Read the `ZEALT_RUN_ID` environment variable. Use it as `run-id` throughout.
* Upload exactly four files at the root of the OneDrive drive named `APIDECK_FILE_STORAGE_DRIVE_NAME` with names:
  * `KEEP-${run-id}-1.txt`
  * `KEEP-${run-id}-2.txt`
  * `SKIP-${run-id}-1.txt`
  * `SKIP-${run-id}-2.txt`
    Each file's body content can be any small text payload.
* Issue a single `POST /file-storage/files/search` request with body query `KEEP-${run-id}` and collect the ids of the matching files.
* Persist a single JSON object to the log file containing only the ids of the matching `KEEP-...` files.

## Implementation Hints

* Apideck unified requests require `Authorization`, `x-apideck-app-id`, `x-apideck-consumer-id`, and `x-apideck-service-id: onedrive` headers.
* File uploads use the `https://upload.apideck.com` host, with raw binary body and metadata in the `x-apideck-metadata` header. Other endpoints use `https://unify.apideck.com`.
* The drive id can be resolved by listing drives and matching on the `APIDECK_FILE_STORAGE_DRIVE_NAME` env var.
* `POST /file-storage/files/search` accepts a JSON body of the form `{ "query": "..." }`; iterate cursor pages if the response is paginated.
* Output ids must correspond to files returned by the unified File Storage API (the `id` field of each file in the response).

## Acceptance Criteria

* Project path: /home/user/apideck\_task
* Ensure the real upload and search actions are executed against Apideck and the log artifact exists.
* Log file: /home/user/apideck\_task/output.log
* The log file must contain a single line of valid JSON in the exact shape: `{"search_result_ids": ["<id1>", "<id2>"]}` where the array contains exactly the two file ids returned by `POST /file-storage/files/search` for the two `KEEP-${run-id}-*.txt` files (order does not matter).
* After the task, exactly four files matching `KEEP-${run-id}-1.txt`, `KEEP-${run-id}-2.txt`, `SKIP-${run-id}-1.txt`, and `SKIP-${run-id}-2.txt` must exist at the root of the drive named `APIDECK_FILE_STORAGE_DRIVE_NAME`.

# File Search Endpoint with ApiDeck File Storage (OneDrive)

## Background

You are working with the Apideck File Storage Unified API connected to OneDrive. Your job is to seed the configured OneDrive drive with four marker files and then exercise the unified `Search Files` endpoint to retrieve only the relevant subset.

## Requirements

* Read the `ZEALT_RUN_ID` environment variable. Use it as `run-id` throughout.
* Upload exactly four files at the root of the OneDrive drive named `APIDECK_FILE_STORAGE_DRIVE_NAME` with names:
  * `KEEP-${run-id}-1.txt`
  * `KEEP-${run-id}-2.txt`
  * `SKIP-${run-id}-1.txt`
  * `SKIP-${run-id}-2.txt`
    Each file's body content can be any small text payload.
* Issue a single `POST /file-storage/files/search` request with body query `KEEP-${run-id}` and collect the ids of the matching files.
* Persist a single JSON object to the log file containing only the ids of the matching `KEEP-...` files.

## Implementation Hints

* Apideck unified requests require `Authorization`, `x-apideck-app-id`, `x-apideck-consumer-id`, and `x-apideck-service-id: onedrive` headers.
* File uploads use the `https://upload.apideck.com` host, with raw binary body and metadata in the `x-apideck-metadata` header. Other endpoints use `https://unify.apideck.com`.
* The drive id can be resolved by listing drives and matching on the `APIDECK_FILE_STORAGE_DRIVE_NAME` env var.
* `POST /file-storage/files/search` accepts a JSON body of the form `{ "query": "..." }`; iterate cursor pages if the response is paginated.
* Output ids must correspond to files returned by the unified File Storage API (the `id` field of each file in the response).

## Acceptance Criteria

* Project path: /home/user/apideck\_task
* Ensure the real upload and search actions are executed against Apideck and the log artifact exists.
* Log file: /home/user/apideck\_task/output.log
* The log file must contain a single line of valid JSON in the exact shape: `{"search_result_ids": ["<id1>", "<id2>"]}` where the array contains exactly the two file ids returned by `POST /file-storage/files/search` for the two `KEEP-${run-id}-*.txt` files (order does not matter).
* After the task, exactly four files matching `KEEP-${run-id}-1.txt`, `KEEP-${run-id}-2.txt`, `SKIP-${run-id}-1.txt`, and `SKIP-${run-id}-2.txt` must exist at the root of the drive named `APIDECK_FILE_STORAGE_DRIVE_NAME`.

Resolve the drive id by listing drives and matching on APIDECK_FILE_STORAGE_DRIVE_NAME

Persist search result IDs to output.log

input.command:
RUN_ID="zr-drrvvwm"
DRIVE_ID="3ada079b78534ff1"
API_KEY="(*****SECRET_LEAK_DETECTED*****)"
APP_ID="605EzrgGklBBo5T9tcIgkHs4omRyk3ykcJVtcIg"
CONSUMER_ID="test-consumer"

for SUFFIX in "KEEP-${RUN_ID}-1" "KEEP-${RUN_ID}-2" "SKIP-${RUN_ID}-1" "SKIP-${RUN_ID}-2"; do
  FILENAME="${SUFFIX}.txt"
  META=$(printf '{"name":"%s","parent_folder_id":"root","drive_id":"%s"}' "$FILENAME" "$DRIVE_ID")
  echo "Uploading $FILENAME..."
  RESULT=$(curl -s -X POST "https://upload.apideck.com/file-storage/files/upload" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $API_KEY" \
    -H "x-apideck-app-id: $APP_ID" \
    -H "x-apideck-consumer-id: $CONSUMER_ID" \
    -H "x-apideck-service-id: onedrive" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/octet-stream" \
    -H "x-apideck-metadata: $META" \
    -d "content of $FILENAME")
  echo "$RESULT" | python3 -m json.tool 2>/dev/null || echo "$RESULT"
  echo "---"
done

content.text:
Title: Files - File Storage API API Reference

URL Source: https://developers.apideck.com/apis/file-storage/reference#operation/filesUpload

Markdown Content:
Files resource represents files stored in the cloud storage services. It provides methods for managing and accessing the files, such as uploading, downloading, and deleting files. For more information on uploads, refer to the [file upload guide](https://developers.apideck.com/guides/file-upload).

id string

required

A unique identifier for an object.

downstream_id string or null

The third-party API ID of original entity

name string or null

required

The name of the file

description string or null

Optional description of the file

type string or null

required

Enum:file folder url

The type of resource. Could be file, folder or url

path string or null

The full path of the file or folder (includes the file name)

Files example

```
{
  "id": "12345",
  "downstream_id": "12345",
  "name": "sample.jpg",
  "description": "A sample image",
  "type": "file",
  "path": "/Documents/sample.jpg",
  "mime_type": "image/jpeg",
  "size": 1810673,
  "parent_folders": [
    {
      "id": "12345",
      "name": "Personal"
    }
  ],
  "export_formats": [
    "application/pdf",
    "application/vnd.oasis.opendocument.presentation",
    "text/plain"
  ],
  "updated_by": "12345",
  "created_by": "12345",
  "updated_at": "2020-09-30T07:43:32.000Z",
  "created_at": "2020-09-30T07:43:32.000Z"
}
```

### List Files

get

https://unify.apideck.com/file-storage/files

List Files

Authorizations:

#### Header parameters

x-apideck-consumer-id string

required

ID of the consumer which you want to get or push data from

x-apideck-app-id string non-empty

required

The ID of your Unify application

x-apideck-service-id string

Provide the service id you want to call (e.g., pipedrive). Only needed when a consumer has activated multiple integrations for a Unified API.

#### Query parameters

raw boolean

Default:false

Include raw response. Mostly used for debugging purposes

cursor string or null

Cursor to start from. You can find cursors for next/previous pages in the meta.cursors property of the response.

limit integer[ 1 .. 200 ]

Default:20

Number of results to return. Minimum 1, Maximum 200, Default 20

filter object filter[drive_id]=1234

Apply filters

sort object sort[by]=updated_at

Apply sorting

pass_through object pass_through[example_downstream_property]=example_downstream_property

Optional unmapped key/values that will be passed through to downstream as query parameters. Ie: ?pass_through[search]=leads becomes ?search=leads

fields string or null

The 'fields' parameter allows API users to specify the fields they want to include in the API response. If this parameter is not present, the API will return all available fields. If this parameter is present, only the fields specified in the comma-separated string will be included in the response. Nested properties can also be requested by using a dot notation.

Example: `fields=name,email,addresses.city`

In the example above, the response will only include the fields "name", "email" and "addresses.city". If any other fields are available, they will be excluded.

### Responses

200 Files

400 Bad Request

401 Unauthorized

402 Payment Required

404 The specified resource was not found

422 Unprocessable

### Mapping coverage per connector

![Image 1: Box](https://res.cloudinary.com/apideck/icons/box)

Box

![Image 2: Dropbox](https://res.cloudinary.com/apideck/icons/dropbox)

Dropbox

![Image 3: Google Drive](https://res.cloudinary.com/apideck/icons/google-drive)

Google Drive

![Image 4: OneDrive](https://res.cloudinary.com/apideck/icons/onedrive)

OneDrive

![Image 5: SharePoint](https://res.cloudinary.com/apideck/icons/sharepoint)

SharePoint

Request example

```
import { Apideck } from "@apideck/unify";

const apideck = new Apideck({
  consumerId: "test-consumer",
  appId: "dSBdXd2H6Mqwfg0atXHXYcysLJE9qyn1VwBtXHX",
  apiKey: process.env["APIDECK_API_KEY"] ?? "",
});

async function run() {
  const result = await apideck.fileStorage.files.list({
    serviceId: "salesforce",
    filter: {
      driveId: "1234",
      folderId: "root",
      shared: true,
    },
    sort: {
      by: "updated_at",
      direction: "desc",
    },
    passThrough: {
      "search": "San Francisco",
    },
    fields: "id,updated_at",
  });

  for await (const page of result) {
    console.log(page);
  }
}

run();
```

Response example

```
{
  "status_code": 200,
  "status": "OK",
  "service": "google-drive",
  "resource": "files",
  "operation": "all",
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "12345",
      "downstream_id": "12345",
      "name": "sample.jpg",
      "description": "A sample image",
      "type": "file",
      "path": "/Documents/sample.jpg",
      "mime_type": "image/jpeg",
      "downloadable": true,
      "size": 1810673,
      "owner": {
        "id": "12345",
        "email": "hello@apideck.com",
        "name": "Elon Musk"
      },
      "parent_folders": [
        {
          "id": "12345",
          "name": "Personal"
        }
      ],
      "parent_folders_complete": true,
      "permissions": {
        "download": true
      },
      "exportable": true,
      "export_formats": [
        "application/pdf",
        "application/vnd.oasis.opendocument.presentation",
        "text/plain"
```

### Search Files

post

https://unify.apideck.com/file-storage/files/search

Search Files

Authorizations:

#### Header parameters

x-apideck-consumer-id string

required

ID of the consumer which you want to get or push data from

x-apideck-app-id string non-empty

required

The ID of your Unify application

x-apideck-service-id string

Provide the service id you want to call (e.g., pipedrive). Only needed when a consumer has activated multiple integrations for a Unified API.

#### Query parameters

pass_through object pass_through[example_downstream_property]=example_downstream_property

Optional unmapped key/values that will be passed through to downstream as query parameters. Ie: ?pass_through[search]=leads becomes ?search=leads

fields string or null

The 'fields' parameter allows API users to specify the fields they want to include in the API response. If this parameter is not present, the API will return all available fields. If this parameter is present, only the fields specified in the comma-separated string will be included in the response. Nested properties can also be requested by using a dot notation.

Example: `fields=name,email,addresses.city`

In the example above, the response will only include the fields "name", "email" and "addresses.city". If any other fields are available, they will be excluded.

cursor string or null

Cursor to start from. You can find cursors for next/previous pages in the meta.cursors property of the response.

limit integer[ 1 .. 200 ]

Default:20

Number of results to return. Minimum 1, Maximum 200, Default 20

raw boolean

Default:false

Include raw response. Mostly used for debugging purposes

filter object filter[drive_id]=1234

Apply filters

application/json

query string

required

The query to search for. May match across multiple fields.

drive_id string

ID of the drive to filter on

pass_through objects

The pass_through property allows passing service-specific, custom data or structured modifications in request body when creating or updating resources.

### Responses

200 Files

400 Bad Request

401 Unauthorized

402 Payment Required

404 The specified resource was not found

422 Unprocessable

RUN_ID="zr-drrvvwm"
DRIVE_ID="3ada079b78534ff1"
API_KEY="(*****SECRET_LEAK_DETECTED*****)"
APP_ID="605EzrgGklBBo5T9tcIgkHs4omRyk3ykcJVtcIg"
CONSUMER_ID="test-consumer"

for SUFFIX in "KEEP-${RUN_ID}-1" "KEEP-${RUN_ID}-2" "SKIP-${RUN_ID}-1" "SKIP-${RUN_ID}-2"; do
  FILENAME="${SUFFIX}.txt"
  META=$(printf '{"name":"%s","parent_folder_id":"root","drive_id":"%s"}' "$FILENAME" "$DRIVE_ID")
  echo "Uploading $FILENAME..."
  RESULT=$(curl -s -X POST "https://upload.apideck.com/file-storage/files" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $API_KEY" \
    -H "x-apideck-app-id: $APP_ID" \
    -H "x-apideck-consumer-id: $CONSUMER_ID" \
    -H "x-apideck-service-id: onedrive" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/octet-stream" \
    -H "x-apideck-metadata: $META" \
    -d "content of $FILENAME")
  echo "$RESULT" | python3 -m json.tool 2>/dev/null || echo "$RESULT"
  echo "---"
done

input.command:
API_KEY="(*****SECRET_LEAK_DETECTED*****)"
APP_ID="605EzrgGklBBo5T9tcIgkHs4omRyk3ykcJVtcIg"
CONSUMER_ID="test-consumer"
DRIVE_ID="3ada079b78534ff1"
RUN_ID="zr-drrvvwm"

SEARCH_QUERY="KEEP-${RUN_ID}"

echo "Searching for: $SEARCH_QUERY"

# Perform the search, including drive_id filter
curl -s -X POST "https://unify.apideck.com/file-storage/files/search" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $API_KEY" \
  -H "x-apideck-app-id: $APP_ID" \
  -H "x-apideck-consumer-id: $CONSUMER_ID" \
  -H "x-apideck-service-id: onedrive" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "{\"query\": \"${SEARCH_QUERY}\", \"drive_id\": \"${DRIVE_ID}\"}" | python3 -m json.tool

API_KEY="(*****SECRET_LEAK_DETECTED*****)"
APP_ID="605EzrgGklBBo5T9tcIgkHs4omRyk3ykcJVtcIg"
CONSUMER_ID="test-consumer"
DRIVE_ID="3ada079b78534ff1"
RUN_ID="zr-drrvvwm"

# Try listing files at root level of the drive
echo "=== List files at root ==="
curl -s -X GET "https://unify.apideck.com/file-storage/files?filter%5Bdrive_id%5D=${DRIVE_ID}&filter%5Bfolder_id%5D=root&limit=200" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $API_KEY" \
  -H "x-apideck-app-id: $APP_ID" \
  -H "x-apideck-consumer-id: $CONSUMER_ID" \
  -H "x-apideck-service-id: onedrive" | python3 -c "
import sys, json
d = json.load(sys.stdin)
files = d.get('data', [])
for f in files:
    if 'KEEP' in f.get('name','') or 'SKIP' in f.get('name',''):
        print(f'{f[\"name\"]}: {f[\"id\"]}')
print(f'Total files: {len(files)}')
"
